title: 8 Herbal Essential Oils For Hot Flashes
intro_desc: Hot flashes can be a real nuisance, especially for women going through menopause. But did you know that herbal teas can offer some relief? These teas work by calming the body and reducing stress, which can trigger hot flashes. Let's take a look at three herbs that have been shown to be particularly helpful: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, which is a natural sedative and can help regulate body temperature; Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, which is known for its calming effects and can reduce anxiety and stress; and Zingiber officinale, or ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can help ease digestive issues that may contribute to hot flashes.  When you drink a tea made from one of these herbs, the active compounds are absorbed into your bloodstream and work to calm your nervous system. This can lead to a decrease in hot flashes, as well as other symptoms like night sweats and mood swings. By reducing stress and anxiety, these teas can also improve your overall well-being and quality of life. For example, valerian root tea can help you fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly, which can further reduce hot flashes. Lavender tea can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to manage stress and its related symptoms. Ginger tea can ease digestive issues and reduce inflammation, which can also contribute to hot flashes. By incorporating these teas into your daily routine, you may find that you experience fewer hot flashes and a greater sense of calm and well-being.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for hot flashes

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils contains valerenic acid and isovaleric acid as its primary bioactive constituents. These compounds contribute to its sedative and anti-anxiety properties, which can help to reduce the severity and frequency of hot flashes. The sedative properties of valerenic acid can help to calm the nervous system, reducing the body's fight or flight response that can contribute to hot flashes. Isovaleric acid has a soothing effect on the body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger hot flashes. By reducing stress and anxiety, Valeriana officinalis essential oils can provide relief from hot flashes by helping to regulate body temperature and hormone balance.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for hot flashes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis roots, 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba or coconut oil), a glass jar with a lid, and a cheesecloth.
Dry the Valeriana officinalis roots in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 1-2 hours to remove moisture.
Combine the dried roots with the carrier oil in the glass jar, making sure the roots are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. Strain the liquid through the cheesecloth into another container.
Discard the solids and transfer the essential oil to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains a high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, two bioactive constituents that have been found to be effective in reducing hot flashes. Linalool, a terpenoid, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Linalyl acetate, an ester, has a sedative effect and can help to regulate body temperature, further reducing the occurrence of hot flashes. The essential oil also contains beta-caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body. When inhaled, the bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can quickly take effect, providing relief from hot flashes and promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for hot flashes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers in a clean container.
Combine 1 cup of flowers with 2 cups of water in a saucepan, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let simmer for 1 hour, then remove from heat.
Let the mixture steep for 2 hours, then strain it using a cheesecloth into a separate container.
Transfer the liquid to a distillation apparatus, and separate the essential oil from the water using steam distillation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to regulate body temperature and reduce hot flashes. The warming effect of ginger helps to increase blood circulation and calm the nervous system, which can alleviate hot flash symptoms. Additionally, ginger's ability to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances involved in hot flashes, contributes to its effectiveness in managing this issue. By balancing body temperature and hormone levels, Zingiber officinale essential oils can provide relief from hot flashes.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for hot flashes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger roots and 1 cup of water.
Peel the ginger roots and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the ginger pieces and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, then let cool.

Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean bowl, and discard the solids. Use 2 tablespoons of the liquid to dilute 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like jojoba oil) to make a 3% essential oil solution.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help with hot flashes. The oil also contains flavonoids like licochalcone A and B, which have antioxidant properties that can reduce oxidative stress and help regulate body temperature. These properties can help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting a more stable body temperature. Glycyrrhizin also has a hormone-regulating effect, which can help stabilize hormonal imbalances that contribute to hot flashes. By reducing inflammation and regulating body temperature, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils may provide relief from hot flashes.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for hot flashes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. You can purchase them from a health food store or online.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil (such as jojoba or sweet almond oil) to a clean glass jar.
Add 1/4 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots to the jar with the carrier oil.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This will allow the roots to infuse into the oil.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and use the essential oil as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils contains boswellic acid, isorhapontigenin, and avenacoside, which are bioactive constituents that contribute to its benefits. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to regulate body temperature and reduce hot flashes. The antioxidant properties of avenacoside and isorhapontigenin can also help to protect the body from oxidative stress, which is often associated with hormonal imbalances. Boswellic acid has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals, which can contribute to hot flashes. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Avena sativa essential oils may help to alleviate hot flashes and promote overall hormonal balance.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for hot flashes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba oil or coconut oil).
Combine the flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Place the jar in a sunny spot for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day. This allows the oil to extract the plant's properties.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Bottle the extracted oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops in a diffuser or apply topically as needed for hot flashes relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils contains flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, and lignans like lariciresinol and secoisolariciresinol. These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. The lignans in Passiflora incarnata essential oils may also contribute to its hot flash-relieving properties by regulating hormonal balances. The sedative properties of Passiflora incarnata essential oils, which are attributed to the presence of apigenin and linalool, can help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, further reducing hot flash symptoms. By addressing hormonal imbalances and promoting relaxation, Passiflora incarnata essential oils may provide relief from hot flashes in some individuals.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal essential oils for hot flashes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Passiflora incarnata flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Combine the flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the flowers.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Bottle the Passiflora incarnata essential oil and use 5-7 drops in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for skin application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils contains actein, a key bioactive constituent that helps alleviate hot flashes. Actein has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. The essential oil also contains ferulic acid, a compound that has antioxidant properties, which can help regulate body temperature and reduce sweating. Ferulic acid's ability to inhibit the production of stress hormones like cortisol can also contribute to reduced hot flash symptoms. By targeting the underlying causes of hot flashes, Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils can provide natural relief from these symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cimicifuga racemosa herbal essential oils for hot flashes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cimicifuga racemosa roots. Wash them with water and pat dry.
Crush 1/2 cup of the dried roots into small pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Place the crushed roots in a saucepan and add 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba oil).
Heat the mixture over low heat (150-200°F) for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally. Then, let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a glass bottle. Discard the solids and use the oil as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains rosmarinic acid and 1,8-cineole, which are bioactive constituents that help alleviate hot flashes. Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation and prevent cell damage, which contribute to hot flashes. 1,8-cineole has a cooling effect on the body, which can help lower body temperature and provide relief from hot flashes. The antispasmodic properties of Melissa officinalis essential oils also help to relax muscles and reduce sweating, further alleviating symptoms of hot flashes. By reducing inflammation, preventing cell damage, cooling the body, and relaxing muscles, Melissa officinalis essential oils can provide effective relief from hot flashes.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for hot flashes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the Melissa officinalis flowers. Let it steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Transfer the mixture to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil, such as jojoba oil or sweet almond oil.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the bottle daily. After 2-3 weeks, strain and bottle the essential oil.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce hot flashes in some women. Peppermint, chamomile, and black cohosh teas are commonly used for this purpose. These teas are believed to calm the body and regulate hormones. Some women find that drinking these teas before bedtime helps them sleep better and reduces hot flashes during the night.
faq_safe: You can safely consume herbal teas for hot flashes every day. Many herbal teas have natural ingredients that help regulate body temperature. However, it's essential to choose teas made from high-quality, organic ingredients. Some herbal teas may interact with medications or have side effects, so pay attention to how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with hot flashes, but it's hard to predict exactly when you'll notice a difference. Some people start feeling relief after a few days, while others may take a week or two. It's also possible that you may not notice a significant change until after a month of regular consumption.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for hot flashes can be most effective in the morning. This is because it can help regulate your body's temperature throughout the day, making you feel cooler and more comfortable. You can also try it in the afternoon if you're prone to hot flashes at that time.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for hot flashes. Some teas, like black cohosh, may increase the risk of bleeding when taken with blood thinners. St. John's Wort can affect how well certain medications work. Other teas, like valerian, might make medications less effective or more potent, but this is not fully understood.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and experiencing hot flashes, you might be tempted to try herbal teas for relief. However, it's best to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, are generally safe, but others, like raspberry leaf and blue cohosh, may stimulate the uterus and cause complications. It's better to stick with peppermint or ginger tea.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas, especially for hot flashes. Some herbs, like black cohosh and evening primrose, may affect milk supply or interact with medications. However, others like peppermint and ginger are generally considered safe. Choose teas that are specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding mothers, and be sure to consume them in moderation.
